meta.note: Deficiency mechanisms are tags on focus_area nodes, NOT separate hierarchy levels

hierarchy_levels.display_name: Focus Area for Structural Integrity Assessment

deficiency_category_reference.definition: General or localized wall thinning from corrosion, pitting, crevice attack, microbiologically influenced corrosion (MIC), or corrosion under insulation.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Accelerated by low pH, high sulfate/chloride content, and dissolved metals typical of acid mine drainage (AMD); prevalent on wetted steel in clarifiers, reactors, and piping.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Fatigue cracking, stress corrosion cracking, weld/HAZ cracking, thermal fatigue.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Relevant at agitator shafts, rake arm welds, and dosing skid frames subject to cyclic/vibration loading.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Bulging, buckling, dents, distortion, misalignment, impact damage.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Common on silo shells, rake arms, and pipe supports exposed to abrasive slurry or mobile-equipment impact.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Bolted, welded, or flanged connection failures; anchor bolt failure; gasket/seal degradation.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Frequent at flanged chemical piping joints and anchor bolts exposed to corrosive spillage or washdown.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Differential settlement, grout deterioration, drainage-related undermining of foundations.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Elevated risk where structures bear on fill placed over disturbed or reclaimed mine ground.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Cracking, spalling, scaling, sulfate attack, reinforcement corrosion, freeze-thaw damage.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Sulfate attack and reinforcement corrosion risk are elevated by contact with acidic, sulfate-rich mine water in tanks, basins, and foundations.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Wind, snow/ice, UV degradation, external impact, corrosive atmosphere.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Open basins and reactors create a humid, corrosive atmosphere around structural steel and coatings.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Missing components, poor maintenance, debris accumulation, trip hazards.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Applies broadly to walkways, platforms, and access structures across the site.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Unauthorized modification, addition of load without design review.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Watch for field-added platforms, piping, or equipment not reflected in original design basis.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Fire exposure, thermal cycling, thermal shock.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Includes exothermic heat generated during lime slaking, and any electrical/fire risk areas.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Water ingress, roof/wall seal or membrane failure, loss of weatherproofing.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Relevant to chemical storage buildings and roofed control/electrical buildings.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Soil erosion, washed-out backfill, voids from poor drainage or water infiltration.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Heightened around basins/ponds and discharge structures with continuous water contact.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Ground movement (upward heave, cracking) from freeze-thaw cycling in frost-susceptible soils.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Climate-dependent; relevant for shallow foundations in cold-climate sites.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Sudden or local settlement/collapse from inadequate soil bearing capacity.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Higher risk over loosely placed mine backfill or areas with undocumented ground disturbance.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Pile misalignment, breakage, or structural defects in deep foundation elements.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Applies where piles were used to bridge poor or disturbed mine-site ground.
Priority item for chemical containment and any pressurized process/membrane equipment.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Fouling, scaling, blockages, or damage from operating outside design conditions.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Scaling from iron/manganese/gypsum precipitation is common in mine-water treatment process equipment.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Erosion, abrasion, and cavitation from high-velocity or particulate-laden flow.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: A leading damage mechanism here given abrasive lime slurry, HDS recycle, and thickened sludge streams.

deficiency_category_reference.definition: Void migration, subsidence, or sinkhole formation from collapse of underlying abandoned mine workings.
deficiency_category_reference.relevance: Site-specific risk category added for this closed mine site; warrants geotechnical desktop review of historic workings beneath structures.
